## Shared framing: climate projections, uncertainty, and interpretation {background-image="../../shared/assets/images/whale-shark-close.jpg" background-size="cover" background-position="center" .section-photo}

<div class="section-title-panel">
<h2>Shared framing</h2>
<p>How to read the models</p>
</div>

---

## Shared framing {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)
:::

### How to read the models {.slide-title}

::: {.framing-lead}
Before using maps or SDM outputs, we need a shared way to interpret them.
:::

- Projections are “what-if” futures
- Uncertainty comes from the full workflow
- SDMs estimate suitability, not certainty
- Outputs are decision-support tools, not final answers

---

## Projection not prediction {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)
:::

### Projection ≠ prediction {.slide-title}

::: {.projection-layout}
::: {.definition-card}
**Prediction**

“This is what will happen.”
:::

::: {.definition-card}
**Projection**

“This is what could happen under these assumptions.”

::: {.projection-figure}
![](../../shared/assets/images/tree-2023-projection-scenarios.png)

Conceptual schematic adapted from Schoeman et al. (2023), *Trends in Ecology & Evolution*. doi:[10.1016/j.tree.2023.04.005](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tree.2023.04.005)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.key-message}
For this workshop, climate projections help us explore plausible futures, not identify one exact future.
:::

---

## Where uncertainty enters {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)
:::

### Where uncertainty enters {.slide-title}

::: {.framing-lead}
Uncertainty comes from the full chain.
:::

<div class="uncertainty-figure-wrap">
<img class="uncertainty-figure" src="../../shared/assets/images/uncertainty-workflow.svg" alt="Three sources of uncertainty across the modeling workflow.">
</div>

::: {.key-message}
Uncertainty is not failure; it tells us where to be cautious.
:::

---

## Climate-side uncertainty {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)
:::

### Climate-side uncertainty {.slide-title}

- Scenario: which future climate pathway?
- Model: which global climate model?
- Run: which realization / initial conditions?
- Downscaling: how do we move from coarse to regional?
- Depth / variables: surface, mid-water, seafloor, oxygen, temperature, currents, etc.

---

## Biological and SDM uncertainty {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)
:::

### Biological and SDM uncertainty {.slide-title}

- Records: where do we have data, and where are gaps?
- Sampling bias: absent, or just not sampled?
- Predictors: which variables define the niche?
- Algorithm: which statistical approach?
- Suitability: suitable does not always mean present, abundant, or critical habitat.

---

## What SDMs show {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)
:::

### SDMs estimate environmental suitability {.slide-title}

They do not directly show:

::: {.not-list}
- confirmed presence
- abundance
- reproduction
- population trends
- management priority by themselves
:::

::: {.key-message}
Suitable ≠ occupied ≠ important habitat
:::

---

## How we use the outputs {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)
:::

### Use model outputs as decision-support {.slide-title}

They can help identify:

- possible climate exposure
- future suitable areas
- data gaps
- areas needing monitoring
- places where planning may need to be adaptive

::: {.key-message}
Interpret outputs with expert knowledge, local context, and governance feasibility.
:::

---

## Workshop rule {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)
:::

### The goal is not to find “the perfect map” {.slide-title}

The goal is to ask:

- What patterns are consistent?
- What looks uncertain?
- What needs expert review?
- What can improve in the next version?
